blog.title: CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works

facebook.caption:
CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works

Local service businesses do not need a bloated sales platform first. They need a simple handoff stack: capture the source, qualify the request, confirm the next step, and route the right leads to a human fast.

Read it: https://eb28.co/blog/crm-automation-for-local-service-businesses-the-simple-stack-that-works/

instagram.caption:
CRM automation for local service business: start with the handoff after the form.

Capture source page, service need, urgency, and the next action. Then route high-intent leads to a real person with context.

Full guide at eb28.co/blog/

linkedin.caption:
Refreshed EB28 growth brief: CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works

This breaks down the practical handoff stack local service companies need before they buy a heavier CRM: source capture, qualification, instant confirmation, owner-controlled routing, and reporting that ties leads back to the page that created them.

https://eb28.co/blog/crm-automation-for-local-service-businesses-the-simple-stack-that-works/

shortFormVideo.hook: Your CRM does not need to be complicated to stop missed local leads.
script: Show a website form, chat, and missed call landing at the same time.
Cut to four fields: source page, service need, urgency, and next action.
Show instant confirmation going to the lead and a contextual handoff going to the owner.
Close with the guide URL and EB28 project brief CTA.
shortFormVideo.caption: Start CRM automation with the lead handoff, not a bloated sales platform. Guide: eb28.co/blog/
